Acquisitions & M&A.pdf_text: March 18, 2026 To Listing Compliance Department Listing Compliance Department BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Exchange Plaza, Plot No. C/1. G Block, Dalal Street, Bandra -Kurla Complex, Bandra (East), Mumbai - 400 001. Mumbai- 400051. Scrip Code: 543280 Scrip Symbol: NAZARA Dear Sir/Madam, Subject: Intimation of Press Release Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 as amended, please find enclosed herewith the Press Release dated March 18, 2026, titled “Nazara to Acquire Controlling Stake in Social Gaming Platform Bluetile and BestPlay for USD 100.3 million (~INR 918 crores) Positions Nazara at the forefront of AI-driven gaming through an integrated content, engagement and distribution platform.” This is for your information and records. Thanking you. Yours faithfully, For Nazara Technologies Limited Arun Bhandari Company Secretary and Compliance Officer Encl. As aboveNazara to Acquire Controlling Stake in Social Gaming Platform Bluetile and BestPlay for USD 100.3 million (~INR 918 crores) Positions Nazara at the forefront of AI-driven gaming through an integrated content, engagement and distribution platform March 18, 2026: Mumbai, India - Nazara Technologies Limited (BSE: 543280 | NSE: NAZARA) today announced that its wholly owned subsidiary, Nazara Technologies UK Limited (“Nazara UK”), has signed definitive agreements to acquire a 50% controlling stake in Bluetile Games S.L. (formerly known as Playvalve S.L.) and BestPlay Systems S.L. for a consideration of USD 100.3 million (INR ~918 crores). Business Overview Bluetile operates a diversified portfolio of globally popular casual, social mobile games including titles such as Yatzy, Domino Legends, Mahjong Voyage and Spade Stars. The platform has achieved global scale with nearly 375 million downloads and 22 million monthly active users across 17 live games. BestPlay, i
Acquisitions & M&A.clean_headline: Nazara Technologies to acquire controlling stake in social gaming platforms Bluetile and BestPlay for USD 100.3 million (~INR 918 crores)

Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For Belrise Industries Limited Manish Kumar Head of Legal Company Secretary and Compliance Officer Membership No. F7990 Encl: m/aPress Release Belrise Industries Limited (Formerly known as Badve Engineering Limited) Belrise Industries Announces Strategic Acquisition of Chester Hall Precision Engineering, United Kingdom Marks strategic entry into the global aerospace, space and defence precision engineering supply chain National, 18th Mar 2026: Belrise Industries Limited (BIL), one of India’s leading integrated automotive component manufacturers with a diverse portfolio of safety-critical systems and engineering solutions announced that its step-down wholly owned subsidiary, Belrise UK Holdings Limited, has signed a definitive Share Purchase Agreement to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Chester Hall Precision Engineering Holdings Limited (hereafter referred to as Chester Hall) - a United Kingdom- based precision engineering group serving the global aerospace, space and defence sectors. J. Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400 051. Mumbai - 400 001. Symbol: - CENTRUM Scrip Code: - 501150 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub.: Intimation under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 This is further to our letter no. CCL/SEC/2025-26/36 dated August 22, 2025 wherein it was informed that the Company has entered into a Share Purchase Agreement (“SPA”) for transfer of its entire stake in its material subsidiary viz., Centrum Housing Finance Limited (“CHFL”), to Weaver Services Private Limited (“Weaver Services”), subject to the requisite statutory, regulatory and corporate approvals, and certain conditions precedent. We had received regulatory approval from the Reserve Bank of India (“RBI”) for the proposed change in control on February 3, 2026 as disclosed vide letter no CCL/SEC/2025-26/59 on February 4, 2026. We hereby inform you that the Company completed the sale of its entire stake in CHFL on March 18, 2026. Pursuant to the above, CHFL will cease to be a subsidiary of the Company. You are requested to take the above information on record. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For Centrum Capital Limited Shailendra Apte Chief Financial Officer Centrum Capital Limited (CIN No.: L65990MH1977PLC019986) Registered and Corporate Office: Level - 9, Centrum House, C.S.T. Road, Vidyanagari Marg, Kalina, Santacruz (East), Mumbai – 400 098. Tel: +91 22 4215 9000 Email: info@centrum.co.in Website: www.centrum.co.in

Acquisitions & M&A.pdf_text: Date: 18.03.2026 To The Chief General Manager Listing Operation, BSE Limited, 20th Floor, P.J.Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400 001. Ref: Scrip Code 541144 (Active Clothing Co Limited Sub: Pursuant to Reg. 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended (“SEBI LODR Regulations”), Announcement of Allotment of 20,00,000 Convertible Share warrants through preferential basis at a Price of Rs.115/- per warrant, aggregating to Rs. 23,00,00,000.00 Dear Sir/Ma’am, Pursuant to the approval of the Board of Directors at its meeting held on January 27th, 2026 and approval of the members of the Company at their Extra-Ordinary General Meeting held on February 26, 2026 for Preferential issue of convertible warrants, further pursuant to In-principle approval granted by BSE Limited vide their respective letter dated March 11th, 2026, the Issue Committee meeting of the board of the Company held today 18.03.2026 at 01:00 PM has considered and approved the allotment of 20,00,000 (Twenty Lakhs) warrants on preferential basis at an issue price of Rs. 115/- per warrant (includes Face value of Rs.10/- per warrant and Premium of Rs. 105/- per warrant), to Promoters and Non Promoters Group. The Company has received an amount aggregating to Rs. 5,75,00,000/- (Rupees Five Crores Seventy five Lakhs only) at the rate of Rs.115/- per warrant (being 25% of the issue price per warrant) as upfront payment as per Terms of issue. Each of the Warrant, so allotted, is convertible into or exchangeable for one fully paid-up equity share of face value of Rs. 10/- (Rupees ten only) each, at a premium of Rs. 105/- per share of the Company for each warrant, in accordance with the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, (as amended), subject to receipt of balance consideration of Rs.86.25/- per warrant (being 75% of the issue price per warrant) from the allottees to exercise conversion opt

Mukherjee Road, 3rd floor, Fort, Mumbai – 400001 Bhawanipore, Kolkata – 700025 Email: corp.relations@bseindia.com Email: tradevisco@gmail.com Sub: Disclosure under Regulation 29(2) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 ("SEBI SAST Regulations”). Dear Sir/Madam, I, Raj Goenka, belonging to the promoter group of M/s. GAMCO LIMITED („Target Company‟) have acquired 12631 Equity Shares of the face value of Rs. 2/- each (“Equity Shares”) of the Target Company, through open market transaction from 16.03.2026 to 17.03.2026 resulting to total of 1171949 Equity Shares of Rs. 2/- each i.e. 1.98% of the issued and paid-up equity share capital of the Target Company. As required under Regulation 29(2) of the SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeover) Regulations, 2011 we enclose a disclosure with respect to change of my shareholding or voting rights in GAMCO LIMITED. This letter is intended for the information and records of the Target Company and the Stock Exchange. Thanking You, Yours sincerely, (Raj Goenka) Acquirer/PromoterRAJ GOENKA SATYAM TOWERS, 3 ALIPORE ROAD, KOLKATA- 700027, WEST BENGAL Email: goldenit2018@gmail.com Disclosure under Regulation 29(2) of SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 Name of the Target Company (TC) GAMCO LIMITED BSE Scrip Code: 540097 Name(s) of the acquirer and Persons Acting Mrs. Raj Goenka in Concert (PAC) with the acquirer Whether the acquirer belongs to Promoter/ Yes, the acquirer belongs to Promoter Group. Financial Results.pdf_text: 18th March 2026 BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Limited, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, 5th Floor, Dalal Street, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Mumbai 400 001 Bandra (E), Mumbai 400 051 Equity Scrip code: 532343 Equity Scrip code: TVSMOTOR NCRPS Scrip code: 717506 NCRPS Scrip code: TVSMNCRPS Dear Sir / Madam, Subject : Convening of Board meeting, inter-alia, for declaration of interim dividend for the financial year ending 31st March 2026 A meeting of the board of directors of the Company is being convened on Tuesday, the 24th March 2026 inter-alia to consider and declare interim dividend, if any, to the shareholders of the Company for the year ending 31st March 2026. This notice is being given in terms of Regulation 29 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. The rate, quantum, record date and date of payment of interim dividend, if declared, will be advised to you immediately after the board meeting. In view of the above, the trading window for dealing in the Company’s scrips by the Designated Persons of the Company including their immediate relatives remains closed from 18th March 2026 to 26th March 2026 (both days inclusive), in terms of the Company’s Code of Conduct to regulate, monitor and report trading by Designated Persons, read with SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015. Thanking you, Yours truly, For TVS MOTOR COMPANY LIMITED K S Srinivasan Company Secretary Website: www.tvsmotor.com Email: contactus@tvsmotor.com CIN: L35921TN1992PLC022845

Mergers & Schemes.pdf_text: CIN: L65920MH1994PLC080618 HDFC Bank Limited, Email: shareholder.grievances@hdfc.bank.in HDFC House, Website: www.hdfc.bank.in H T Parekh Marg, 165-166 Backbay Reclamation, Churchgate, Mumbai- 400 020 Tel. No.: 022-66316000 Ref.no. SE/2025-26/200 March 18, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Dept. of Corporate Services The Listing Department Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street, Fort, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai – 400 001 Mumbai – 400 051 Scrip Code: 500180 Scrip Symbol: HDFCBANK Dear Sir/ Madam, Sub: Intimation under Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI Listing Regulations”) – resignation of Mr. Atanu Chakraborty as a Part-time Chairman and Independent Director of HDFC Bank Limited (“the Bank”) and appointment of Mr. Keki Mistry as an interim Part-time Chairman of the Bank This is to inform you that Mr. Atanu Chakraborty (DIN: 01469375) has today i.e. on March 18, 2026, tendered his resignation as the Part-time Chairman and Independent Director of the Bank with immediate effect, for reasons mentioned in the resignation letter. The said letter of resignation is annexed herewith. We confirm that there are no reasons other than those mentioned in the said letter, for the resignation of Mr. Chakraborty. Further, Mr. Chakraborty does not hold directorship in any other company. The Board of Directors places on record its appreciation for Mr. Chakraborty’s contribution to the Bank during his tenure and wishes him all the success in his future endeavours. Further, based on an application made by the Bank in this regard, the Reserve Bank of India on March 18, 2026, has granted its approval for the appointment of Mr. Keki Mistry (DIN: 00008886) as an interim Part- time Chairman of the Bank with effect from March 19, 2026, for a period of 3 months. This is for your information and appropriate dissemination. Yours sincerely, For HDFC Bank Limited Ajay Agarwal Company Secretary

Fund Raise / QIP / NCD.headline: Reason for Delay in Disclosure - Demise of Independent Director
Fund Raise / QIP / NCD.pdf_text: 18th March, 2026 To BSE Limited Listing Department Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400 001 Script Code: 531540 Subject: Submission of Reason for Delay in Disclosure – Demise of Independent Director Dear Sir/Madam, This is with reference to your communication seeking additional details under SEBI Circular dated November 11, 2024, regarding our corporate announcement filed on 17th March 2026 under Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015, pertaining to the demise of our Independent Director. We would like to submit that the delay in disclosure was inadvertent and purely due to the fact that the Company was not aware of the unfortunate demise of the Independent Director within the stipulated 24-hour timeframe. The information was conveyed to the Company subsequently, following which we made the necessary disclosure to the Exchange at the earliest possible instance. There was no malafide intention or deliberate delay in making the disclosure. We regret the delay and assure you of our commitment to compliance with regulatory requirements. We will take necessary steps to ensure timely disclosures in the future. We request you to kindly take the above explanation on record. The details as required under Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015 are enclosed herewith as Annexure-A Yours faithfully, For Maruti Infrastructure Limited Nimesh D Patel Chairman & Managing Director -Annexure-A Sr. Requirement Particular No. 1 Reason of change(i.e. appointment, Demise resignation, removal, death or otherwise) 2 Date of Appointment / cessation (as 15th March 2026 applicable) and terms of appointment 3 Brief Profile Not Applicable 4 Disclosure of relationships between Not Applicable Director -

Fund Raise / QIP / NCD.headline: Outcome of the Rights Issue Committee ("Committee") of Bluegod Entertainment Limited for Extension of Closing date of Rights Issue
Fund Raise / QIP / NCD.pdf_text: BLUEGOD ENTERTAINMENT LIMITED CIN: L74202MP1984PLC002592 Registered Office: 301-G Goyal Vihar, Gate No. 2 Khajrana Road, Indore (M.P). Email id- hello@bluegod.in, Website: https://bluegod.in/ Tel. 7383380911 Date: 18th March, 2026 To, Listing Department BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001, Maharashtra, India Ref: ISIN: INE924N01024, Scrip Code: 539175, Symbol: BLUEGOD Subject: Outcome of the Rights Issue Committee ("Committee") of Bluegod Entertainment Limited for Extension of Closing date of Rights Issue. Dear Sir/Ma’am, With reference to the Outcome of the Meeting of the Board of Directors held on February 28, 2026, the issue closure date has been updated from Thursday, March 19, 2026 to Friday, March 20, 2026. This is to update that the date of closure of the Rights Issue, which opened on Thursday, March 12, 2026 and was scheduled to close on Thursday, March 19, 2026, has now been further extended from Thursday, March 19, 2026, to Friday, March 20, 2026, by Rights Issue Committee in its Meeting held on Wednesday, March 18, 2026 in order to provide an opportunity to shareholders to exercise their rights in the Rights Issue. Accordingly, the last date of submission of the duly filled in CAF (along with the amount payable on application) is Friday, March 20, 2026. Equity Shareholders of the Company who are entitled to apply for the Rights Issue as mentioned above are requested to take note of the Issue Closure Date as Friday, March 20, 2026. Accordingly, there is no change in the LOF and CAF dated Saturday, February 28, 2026. except for change in Issue Closing Date and resultant change in indicative time table of post issue activities on account of change in Issue Closing Date. Activity Schedule Dates Issue Opening Date Thursday, March 12, 2026 Issue Closing Date Friday, March 20, 2026 The committee commenced at 10:00 A.M. and concluded at 11:00 A.M. You are requested to take the above on record and acknowledge Yours truly, Yours Fai

Towers, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (East) Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400051 Mumbai - 400001 Symbol - TCS Scrip Code No. - 532540 Dear Sirs, Sub: Press Release We are sending herewith copy of the Press Release titled “TCS and Pearson partner to accelerate AI-powered learning for global industries” which will be disseminated shortly. The Press release is self-explanatory. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For Tata Consultancy Services Limited Yashaswin Sheth Company Secretary ACS 15388 Encl: as above 9th Floor Nirmal Building Nariman Point Mumbai 400 021 Tel 91 22 6778 9595 Fax 91 22 6630 3672 e-mail corporate.office@tcs.com website www.tcs.com Registered Office 9th Floor Nirmal Building Nariman Point Mumbai 400 021 Corporate Identity No. (CIN): L22210MH1995PLC084781TCS and Pearson partner to accelerate AI-powered learning for global industries The multi‑year partnership will help enterprises build future‑ready workforces by combining AI‑powered learning, assessment, and cloud‑led transformation at scale LONDON, UK | MUMBAI, INDIA, 18 March 2026: Pearson (FTSE: PSON.L), the world’s lifelong learning company, and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) (BSE: 532540, NSE: TCS), a global leader in IT services and consulting, have announced a multi-year integrated partnership to help enterprises build future-ready workforces with AI-powered learning and assessment. Through this collaboration, Pearson will bring its enterprise learning and assessment expertise together with TCS’ leadership in Contextual AI and the robust TCS iON digital learning and assessment platforms. Together, the companies will empower enterprises to build a perpetually adaptive workforce, leveraging AI-driven insights to bridge skill gaps and drive measurable business innovation. Scrip Code: 539682 Subject: Outcome of Meeting of Board of Directors held on Wednesday, March 18, 2026. Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 read with Part A of Schedule III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“Listing Regulations”), we wish to inform you that the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held today, i.e. on Wednesday, March 18, 2026, inter-alia, considered and approved the following matters: 1. Sub-division/split of 1 (one) equity share of face value of Rs. 10/- (Rupees Ten Only) each, fully paid- up, into 5 (Five) equity shares of face value of Re. 2/- (Rupee Two Only) each, fully paid- up, subject to the approval of the shareholders of the Company and such other regulatory / statutory approvals as may be required. The record date for the purpose of the sub-division shall be intimated in due course. Details as required to be furnished under Regulation 30 read with Schedule III Part A of Listing Regulations and SEBI Master Circular No. HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated January 30, 2026, are enclosed as Annexure-A. 2. Alteration of the Memorandum of Association of the Company, as set out below, subject to the approval of the shareholders of the Company: a) Alteration of Capital Clause (Clause V) pursuant to the sub-division / split of equity shares of the Company; andb) Alteration of Objects Incidental or Ancillary to the Attainment of the Main Objects (Clause III (B)). Details as required to be furnished under the Regulation 30 read with Schedule III Part A of Listing Regulations and SEBI Master Circular No. HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated January 30, 2026, is enclosed as Annexure-B. 3. Ref: Ontic Finserve Limited BSE SCRIP CODE: 540386 SCRIP ID: ONTIC Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“Listing Regulations”), we wish to inform you that Mr. Vishwambar Kameshwar Singh (DIN: DIN: 09822587) vide his letter dated March 18, 2026, have tendered his resignation from the position of Director of the Company. Management Changes.pdf_text: TO ALL STOCK EXCHANGES BSE LIMITED NATIONAL STOCK EXCHANGE OF INDIA LIMITED NEW YORK STOCK EXCHANGE March 18, 2026 Dear Sir/ Madam, Sub: Press release Please find enclosed the press release titled “Ethisphere recognizes Infosys among 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies®” This information will also be hosted on the Company’s website, at www.infosys.com. This is for your information and records. Yours Sincerely, For Infosys Limited A.G.S. Manikantha Company Secretary Membership No: A21918PRESS RELEASE Ethisphere recognizes Infosys among 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® Infosys honored for its commitment to business integrity through robust ethics, compliance, and governance programs. Bengaluru, India – March 18, 2026: Infosys (NSE, BSE, NYSE: INFY), a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting, has been recognized as one of the 2026 World’s Most Ethical Companies® by Ethisphere, a global leader in defining and advancing the standards of ethical business practices. Infosys has been recognized for the sixth consecutive year and is one of only a select group of honorees in the Technology industry. In 2026, 138 honorees were recognized, spanning 17 countries and 40 industries. These companies were evaluated based on the Ethisphere Ethics Quotient®, a questionnaire covering 240 different proof points on the culture of ethics; environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices; ethics and compliance program; diversity, equity, and inclusion; and initiatives that support a strong value chain. “Congratulations to Infosys for achieving recognition as one of the World’s Most Ethical Companies®. As we mark the 20th class of honorees, this group continues to raise the bar for business integrity by embedding ethics into everyday decision-making and long-term strategy. Companies with strong ethics, compliance, and governance programs are built for better long-term performance,” said Erica Salmon Byrne, Ethisphere’s Chief Strategy Officer and Executive C

Management Changes.pdf_text: ` Date: March 18, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, C-1, Block G, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai – 400001 India Mumbai – 400051 India Scrip Code: 543529 Symbol: DELHIVERY Sub: Press Release- Delhivery International Launches Economy Air Shipping to UK, Canada, and Australia Dear Sir/ Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, please find enclosed herewith a copy of the press release titled “Delhivery International Launches Economy Air Shipping to UK, Canada, and Australia”. The press release will also be hosted on the Company’s website at www.delhivery.com. You are requested to take the above on record. Thank you. Yours sincerely, For Delhivery Limited Madhulika Rawat Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Membership No.: F8765 Encl: As aboveDelhivery International Launches Economy Air Shipping to UK, Canada, and Australia BENGALURU, India – March 18, 2026: Delhivery today announced the expansion of its Economy Air Parcel Service under ‘Delhivery International’ to the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia. This launch is strategically timed to coincide with a transformative era in Indian foreign trade, particularly as the India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is expected to go live in the coming months, alongside ongoing trade negotiations with Canada and Australia. Following the December 2025 launch of Economy Air Parcel Service to the USA, this expansion to the United Kingdom, Canada, and Australia significantly expands access for Indian MSMEs and enterprise exporters shipping to consumers in these large markets. The addition of these regions to Delhivery's network addresses the demand for Indian MSME exports such as apparel, handicrafts, and electronics, which are expected to see significant boost under the new trade agreements. The service utilizes Delhivery’s domestic infrastructure to connect India’s Tie

Management Changes.headline: Zydus and Torrent Pharma sign licensing agreement to co-market innovative Semaglutide Injection in India.
Management Changes.pdf_text: March 18, 2026 BSE Limited Code: 532321 P J Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 National Stock Exchange of India Limited Code: Zyduslife Exchange Plaza, C/1, Block G, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Bandra (East), Mumbai-400051 Re.: Press Release Dear Sir / Madam, Please find enclosed a copy of press release dated March 18, 2026, titled “Zydus and Torrent Pharma sign licensing agreement to co-market innovative Semaglutide Injection in India”. The contents of the press release give full details. Please bring the aforesaid news to the notice of the members of the exchange and the investors’ at large. Yours faithfully, For, Zydus Lifesciences Limited Dhaval N. Soni Company Secretary and Compliance Officer Membership No. FCS7063 Encl.: As aboveZydus and Torrent Pharma sign licensing agreement to co-market innovative Semaglutide Injection in India  Zydus has developed an innovative formulation of Semaglutide Injection (15 mg/3 ml) in a prefilled cartridge administered using a patient-friendly reusable pen device  Zydus will market the product under the brand names – SEMAGLYNTM, MASHEMATM and ALTERMETM  Torrent will market the product under the brand name SEMBOLICTM  Partnership aims to expand access for patients with insufficiently controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus and for chronic weight management Ahmedabad, 18 March, 2026 Zydus Lifesciences Limited (“Zydus”), an innovation-led global life-sciences company and Torrent Pharmaceuticals Limited (“Torrent Pharma”), a specialty-focused global pharmaceutical company, have entered into a Licensing and Supply Agreement to co-market Semaglutide Injection in the Indian market. Under the terms of the agreement, Zydus will manufacture and supply the product, while Torrent Pharma has received semi-exclusive rights to co-market the product in India under its own brand name SEMBOLICTM, leveraging its strong presence in chronic therapies and extensive field force across the country. Zydus will market the product under its brand SEMAGLYNTM,

Management Changes.headline: ION Exchange (India) Limited has informed the Exchange regarding Outcome of Board Meeting held on March 18, 2026.
Management Changes.pdf_text: March 18, 2026 To BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited The Corporate Relationship Dept. Exchange Plaza, C-1, Block- G, P.J. Towers, Dalal Street Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (East), Mumbai-400 001 Mumbai-400 051 Scrip Code: 500214 Symbol: IONEXCHANG Sub: Outcome of the Board Meeting under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI Listing Regulations, 2015”) Dear Sir/ Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 read with Schedule III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we wish to inform you that, based on the recommendation of the Nomination and Remuneration Committee, the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held today, i.e., Wednesday, March 18, 2026, has, inter alia, considered and approved the adoption of the “Ion Exchange (India) Limited – Employee Stock Option Scheme, 2026” (“ESOP 2026”) and its implementation through an irrevocable employee welfare trust to be set up by the Company, in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the SEBI (Share Based Employee Benefits and Sweat Equity) Regulations, 2021, subject to the approval of the shareholders of the Company and such other regulatory/statutory approvals as may be necessary. The Board further approved the reconstitution of the Employee Stock Option Committee as follows: Sr. No. Name Designation 1. Mrs. Alka Arora Misra Chairperson 2. Mr. David Rasquinha Member 3. Mr. Sanjay Joshi Member The details required pursuant to SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD- POD2/I/3762/2026 dated January 30, 2026 are provided in the Annexure A. The meeting of the Board of Directors of the Company commenced at 12:15 p.m. and concluded at 5:30 p.m.The above information is available on the website of the Company at www.ionexchangeglobal.com. Kindly take the information on record. Thanking You, Yours faithfully, For Ion Exchange (India) Limited Nikisha Solanki Company Secretary & Co

Regulatory & BSE Queries.headline: For more information, please refer attachment.
Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: Date: March 18, 2026 To, To, BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, C-1, Block G, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400 001 Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai – 400 051 Scrip Code: 532832 Symbol: EMBDL Subject: Disclosure under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI LODR Regulations”) — Order passed by the Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board in respect of land held by a subsidiary of the Company Dear Sir / Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI LODR Regulations read with Schedule III thereto, we wish to inform you that the Chief Executive Officer and Executive Member, Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board ("KIADB"), Bangalore, has passed an order dated March 16, 2026, received by EEBP on March 17, 2026, in relation to the land admeasuring approx. 78 acres situated at Kadugodi Industrial Area, Bengaluru Urban District (“Land”) held by Embassy East Business Park Limited ("EEBP "), a subsidiary of the Company. I. BACKGROUND EEBP holds leasehold rights on the Land pursuant to a Lease Cum Sale Agreement (“LCSA”) dated June 7, 2007, executed with KIADB, which is valid up to June 6, 2029. KIADB has passed an order dated March 16, 2026 under Section 34B of the Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Act, 1966 (“Order”), directing resumption of the Land on account of alleged breaches of the terms of the LCSA, primarily pertaining to execution of memorandum of understanding/ agreements /agreement-to-sell arrangements with third party sub-lessee/ potential buyers in respect of certain portions of the Land, without prior approval of KIADB, and EEBP has been directed to surrender possession of the Land within 30 days from the date of the Order. II. GROUNDS FOR CHALLENGING THE ORDER EEBP denies and refutes the allegations made by KIADB and on the advice of legal counsel, is firmly of the view that the Order can be successfully challenged for the following rea
Regulatory & BSE Queries.clean_headline: Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board passed order dated March 16, 2026 regarding land held by company subsidiary

Regulatory & BSE Queries.raw_headline: Update on First and Final Call - Conversion of partly paid-up equity shares into fully paid-up equity shares, upon receipt of Call money
Regulatory & BSE Queries.headline: Update on First and Final Call - Conversion of partly paid-up equity shares into fully paid-up equity shares, upon receipt of Call money
Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: Date: March 18, 2026 National Stock Exchange of India Limited Exchange Plaza, C-1 Block G Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai - 400051, India Symbol: BHARTIARTL/ AIRTELPP BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400001, India Scrip Code: 532454/ 890157 Sub.: Conversion of partly paid-up equity shares into fully paid-up equity shares, upon receipt of First and Final Call Dear Sir/ Ma’am, This is in furtherance to our earlier announcements regarding the First and Final Call of INR 401.25 per share (comprising INR 3.75 towards face value and INR 397.50 towards premium) (the “First and Final Call”) in respect of the outstanding partly paid-up equity shares of the Company, issued on a rights basis, pursuant to the Letter of Offer dated September 22, 2021 (“Letter of Offer”). We wish to inform you that the Company has received an aggregate amount of INR 156,959.77 million towards First and Final Call on 391,176,994 partly paid-up equity shares from the eligible shareholders. In view of the above, the ‘Special Committee of Directors for Rights Issue’, a duly authorised Committee of the Board of Directors (‘Board’), has today approved the conversion of aforesaid 391,176,994 partly paid-up equity shares of face value of INR 5 each (paid-up value of INR 1.25 each) bearing ISIN IN9397D01014, into fully paid-up equity shares of face value of INR 5 each under existing ISIN INE397D01024. The converted shares shall rank pari passu in all respects with the existing fully paid-up equity shares and shall be available for trading on stock exchanges namely National Stock Exchange of India Limited and BSE Limited, under ISIN INE397D01024, subject to completion of necessary formalities including corporate actions and receipt of requisite listing and trading approvals from stock exchanges. Consequent to the above conversion, the paid-up equity share capital of the Company stands increased to INR 30,467,799,900, divided into 6,093,282,313 fully paid-up equity share

Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: (Under Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process) March 18, 2026 The Manager, The Manager, BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Limited, Listing Department, Listing Department, Exchange Plaza, 5th Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, floor, Plot no. C/1, G Block, BKC Dalal Street, Mumbai 400001 Bandra (East), Mumbai 400051 Scrip Code: 514034 Symbol: JBFIND Sub: Disclosure under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 — Rejection of Resolution Plan Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“the Listing Regulations”) read with of Para A - Part A of Schedule III of the Listing Regulations, it is hereby informed that the Resolution Professional had submitted 1 (one) Resolution Plan before the Hon’ble National Company Law Tribunal (“NCLT”), Ahmedabad Bench in the matter of CIRP of JBF Industries Limited (“the Company”). Further, pursuant to Regulation 30 of the Listing Regulations read with Para A - Part A of Schedule III of the Listing Regulations, it is also informed that the Hon’ble NCLT, Ahmedabad Bench has orally pronounced an order on March 18, 2026, rejecting the said Resolution Plan in respect of the Company under Section 31 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016, as amended, ("IBC") and referring the matter back to the COC. Please take the same on your records and suitably disseminate to all concerned. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For JBF Industries Limited Mr. Mukesh Verma Resolution Professional (RP) Registration No: IBBI/IPA-001/IP-P01665/2019-2020/12522 Regd Office : Shop No.4, Ground Floor, Building No.A, Shubh Laxmi Complex, Near Prabhat School Chanandevi, Amli, Silvassa, Dadra & Nagar Haveli – 396230, Earlier Regd Office : 1st Floor, Building No.B-2, Tirupati Residency, Tirupati Balaji Temple, Basera Road, Silvassa, Dadra & Nagar Haveli – 396230, CIN : L99999DN1982PLC000128 Tel ; +91 6356020333 E-mail : cirp.jbf@gmail

Ltd., Twelve Lakh Seventy Thousand Four Hundred and Forty) subsidiary of the Company The above notices allege short payment of GST. Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: BLO LAMINAT Date: 18.03.2026 To, The Secretary, Listing Department BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai - 400 001 Sub: Outcome of the 21st Committee of Creditors Meeting ("COC") of MIs Bloom Dekor Limited ("Corporate Debtor" or "BDL") which is currently undergoing Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process ("CIRP") Ref: Disclosure under Regulation 30(2) read with sub-clause 16(g) of Para-A of Part-A of Schedule III of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations & Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. Dear SirlMadam, With reference to the captioned matter, this is to inform you that the 2151 Meeting of CoC of Mis Bloom Dekor Limited-In CIRP was successfully convened on Wednesday, ll'h March 2026 wherein various matters were discussed as per the requirement of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016, key (material) decisions are as follows: Resolutions which were approved by 100% majority: ~ Approved the Resolution Professional to file an Interlocutory Application before the Adjudicating Authority for exclusion of the litigation period pending before the Hon'ble NCLAT till the disposal of the order dated 13.01.2026 and to grant extension of the CIRP period by 120 days, along with condonation of delay. Further, approved the appointment of Thakkar and Pahwa, Advocates for filing the aforesaid Interlocutory Application at a professional fee ofRs. 25,0001-plus OPE on actual basis, with the said expenses forming part of the CIRP Costs. ~ Approved and ratified the extension granted to the Resolution Applicants (RAs) for submission of the revised Resolution Plan till 16.03 .2026, subject to the approval of the Interlocutory Application for exclusion and extension of the CIRP period to be filed before the Hon'ble NCLT. ~ Approved and ratified the payment of Head Office property tax along with applicable interest and penalty, and confirming that the said expenses shall form part of the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CJRP) Co

Regulatory & BSE Queries.headline: We wish to inform you that the Company has successfully commissioned its Solar Module Manufacturing Facility on March 18, 2026.
Regulatory & BSE Queries.pdf_text: Date: March 18, 2026 To, The Manager-Listing BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai – 400001 Respected Sir/Ma’am, Sub: Disclosure under Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 - Intimation regarding Commissioning of Solar Module Manufacturing Facility in Ahmedabad Ref: Solarium Green Energy Limited (Security Id.: SOLARIUM, Security Code: 544354) Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“SEBI Listing Regulations”), read with Para B of Part A of Schedule III thereto, as amended from time to time, we wish to inform that the Company has successfully commissioned its Solar Module Manufacturing Facility on March 18, 2026. The facility is located at Survey No. 508/1 & 508/2, Village Miroli, Taluka Daskroi, District Ahmedabad, Gujarat – 382425, with an installed manufacturing capacity of 1 GW (1000 MW) per annum. The plant is fully installed, tested, and is ready for commercial production, and capacity utilisation is expected to ramp up gradually over time. Further, the Company had earlier intimated the Stock Exchange regarding re-entry into manufacturing of solar modules and setting up of the said facility vide disclosure dated June 10, 2025, under Regulation 30 of the SEBI Listing Regulations. The commissioning of this facility marks a significant operational milestone and is expected to strengthen the Company’s supply chain and enhance execution capabilities of its EPC business. We request you to kindly take the above information on record. Yours faithfully, For, Solarium Green Energy Limited Ankit Garg Chairman & Managing Director DIN: 08027760 Place: Ahmedabad

Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai-400001 Subject: Intimation of execution of Turbocor® Authorized Service Partner Agreement with Danfoss LLC pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015 Scrip Code: 544458 ISIN: INE0FMZ01045 Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we wish to inform you that Shree Refrigerations Limited (“the Company”) has entered into a Turbocor® Authorized Service Partner Agreement with Danfoss LLC, USA. The Agreement is signed on 17th March 2026, during an agreement signing event at Mumbai. Further, this agreement enables the Company to become a Turbocor Authorised Service Partner (TASP) and undertake repair of the Danfoss Turbocor compressors locally. This will be the first and only such center, presently, for the region. The details of the Agreement as required under Regulation 30 read with Para B of Part A of Schedule III of the SEBI Listing Regulations and SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated 30th January 2026 are provided below: Sr. Particulars Details No. Name(s) of parties with whom the - Shree Refrigerations Limited 1 agreement is entered - Danfoss LLC, USAThis agreement enables the Company to become a Turbocor Authorised 2 Purpose of entering into the agreement Service Partner for undertaking repair of the Danfoss Turbocor compressors. 3 Size of agreement Not Applicable Shareholding, if any, in the entity with 4 Nil whom the agreement is executed Significant terms of the agreement (in brief) This agreement enables the Company special rights like right to appoint directors, to become a Turbocor Authorised 5 first right to share subscription in case of Service Partner for undertaking repair issuance of shares, right to restrict any of the Danfoss Turbocor compressors. change in capital structure

Order Wins.pdf_text: 18th March 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, 5th Floor, Plot No. C/1, Dalal Street, Fort, G Block, Bandra - Kurla Complex, Mumbai- 400001 Bandra (E), Mumbai - 400 051 Scrip code: 511742 NSE Symbol: UGROCAP Dear Sir/ Madam, Subject: Allotment of Non-Convertible Debentures Pursuant to the provisions of Regulation 30 and 51 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we hereby inform you that the Investment and Borrowing Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company (“Committee”) today, i.e. Wednesday, 18th March 2026 has approved allotment of 45,000 (Forty Five Thousand) Listed, Rated, Senior, Secured, Transferable, Redeemable, Non-Convertible Debentures denominated in Indian Rupees ("INR"), each having face value of INR 10,000/- (Indian Rupees Ten Thousand Only) aggregating upto INR 45,00,00,000/- (Indian Rupees Forty Five Crores) through private placement as per the particulars furnished in ‘Annexure’ through resolution by circulation. We request you to take the same on record. Order Wins.raw_headline: SCI has executed a Shipbuilding Contract with Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) on 18th March 2026 for construction of one (1 no.) 3000 DWT Methanol Dual Fuel Platform Supply Vessel (PSV).
Order Wins.headline: SCI has executed a Shipbuilding Contract with Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) on 18th March 2026 for construction of one (1 no.) 3000 DWT Methanol Dual Fuel Platform Supply Vessel (PSV).
Order Wins.pdf_text: Ref: A10-SEC-BD-808/202/2026 Date: 18.03.2026 To, Listing Compliance Department The Manager - Listing Compliance BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, ‘Exchange Plaza’ C-1, Block G, Dalal Street, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai – 400001. Bandra (East), Mumbai – 400051 BSE Scrip Code – 523598 NSE Trading Symbol – SCI Sub: Intimation under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. Dear Sir/ Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 read with Schedule-III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we hereby notify the following: SCI has executed a Shipbuilding Contract with Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd (MDL) on 18th March 2026 for construction of one (1 no.) 3000 DWT Methanol Dual Fuel Platform Supply Vessel (PSV). This PSV will be the first vessel in SCI’s fleet which is planned to operate on alternate fuel Green Methanol, a step towards the green shipping under the aegis of National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM). Date and time of occurrence of event: 18th March 2026 at 1315 hours. Submitted for your information, kindly take the same on your records. Thanking You. Yours faithfully, For The Shipping Corporation of India Limited Smt. Swapnita Vikas Yadav Company Secretary and Compliance Officer
Order Wins.clean_headline: Shipping Corporation of India executes shipbuilding contract with Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders for construction of one 3000 DWT Methanol Dual Fuel Platform Supply Vessel.

Order Wins.pdf_text: Hasti Finance Limited No. 14 Imperial Hotel Complex, Near Albert Cinema Hall, Whannels Road, Egmore, Chennai 600008, Tamil Nadu CIN: L65191TN1994PLC028333 Email Id:hastifinanceltd@gmail.com Date: 18th March 2026 To, To, The Manager - Corporate Service Department BSE Limited, Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Dalal Street, Mumbai- 400 001. Scrip Code : 531387 — Hasti Finance Limited Dear Sir / Madam, Sub: Outcome of Board meeting and Intimation under Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, as amended from time to time, we hereby inform that the Board of Directors of the Company, at its meeting held today,i.e. Wednesday, 18th March, 2026, has approved the appointment of Mr. Ankit Kumar Jha as the Company Secretary and Compliance Officer (Key Managerial Personnel) of the Company with effect from 18th March, 2026. The meeting commenced at 11:30a.m and concluded at 12:05 p.m. The details as required in terms of Regulation 30 read with Part A of Schedule III of the SEBI LODR and SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD-PoD1/P/CIR/2023/123 dated July 13, 2023, is enclosed herewith as Annexure A. For Hasti Finance Limited NITIN Digitally signed by NITIN PRABHUDAS SOMANI PRABHU DAS Date: 2026.03.18 12:05:43 SOMANI +05'30 Nitin Prabhudas Somani Managing Director (DIN: 00841378)Hasti Finance Limited No. 14 Imperial Hotel Complex, Near Albert Cinema Hall, Whannels Road, Egmore, Chennai 600008, Tamil Nadu CIN: L65191TN1994PLC028333 Email Id:hastifinanceltd@gmail.com Annexure- A Disclosure of information under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 read with SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD PoD1/P/CIR/2023/123 dated 13th July, 2023. Disclosure as to amendment to the existing Clause V of the Memorandum of Association of the Company under regulation 30 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015 read with SEBI Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/CFD-PoD-1/P/CIR/2023/123 dated July 13, 2023 and SEBI Circular SEBI/HO/CFD/PoD2/CIR/P/0155 dated November 11, 2024 and is given in attached Annexure B. The EGM concluded at 01: 32 P.M. (IST). This is for your information and records. Insider / SAST.pdf_text: March 18, 2026 IGAL/SECT/3-26/9 To T o National Stock Exchange of India Limited Department of Corporate Services Exchange Plaza, C - 1, Block G BSE Limited Bandra Kurla Complex Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Bandra - (E) Dalal Street Mumbai - 400 051 Mumbai - 400 001 Symbol: INDIGO Scrip Code: 539448 Subject: Disclosure under Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 Dear Sir/ Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 read with Schedule III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we wish to inform that the Company had participated in the bidding process for acquisition of land situated in Sector 29, Gurugram, Haryana. The concerned authority has, vide its Letter of Intent, informed the Company of the proposed allotment of the said land to the Company, subject to completion of applicable terms and conditions. Accordingly, and in compliance with the applicable disclosure requirements, the information required pursuant to SEBI Master Circular No. HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated January 30, 2026, to the extent applicable, is enclosed herewith as Annexure A. Kindly take the above information on record. Thanking you, For InterGlobe Aviation Limited Neerja Sharma Company Secretary & Chief Compliance Officer Encl: a/a InterGlobe Aviation Limited Registered Office: Upper Ground Floor, Thapar House, Gate No. 2, Western Wing, 124 Janpath, New Delhi – 110 001, India. M +91 9650098905, F + 91 11 43513200 Email: corporate@goindigo.in Corporate Office: Emaar Capital Tower-II, Sector-26, Sikanderpur Ghosi, MG Road, Gurugram-122002, Haryana, India. T +91 124 435 2500. CIN no.: L62100DL2004PLC129768Annexure-A Name(s) of parties with whom the agreement is Haryana Shehri Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP) entered This disclosure is being made on the basis of the Letter of intent (LOI) received by the Company from HSVP. The allotment letter will be issued later upon payment of balance purchase c onsideration.

Upcoming Board Meetings.pdf_text: TVS Holdings Limited Registered Office: “Chaitanya”, No. 12, Khader Nawaz Khan Road, [Formerly known as Sundaram-Clayton Limited] Nungambakkam, Chennai – 600006 PH: 044 28332115 18th March 2026 BSE Limited, National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers, Exchange Plaza, 5th Floor, Dalal Street, Bandra-Kurla Complex, Mumbai 400 001. Bandra (E), Mumbai 400 051. Equity Scrip code: 520056 Equity Scrip code: TVSHLTD Dear Sir/Madam, Sub. : Convening of Board meeting, inter-alia, for declaration of interim dividend for the financial year ending 31st March 2026 A meeting of the board of directors of the Company (“the Board”) is being convened on Wednesday, 25th March 2026, inter-alia, to consider and declare interim dividend, if any, to the shareholders of the Company for the year ending 31st March 2026. This notice is being given in terms of Regulation 29 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. The rate, quantum, record date and date of payment of the interim dividend, if declared, will be advised to you immediately after approval of the Board. In view of the above, the trading window for dealing in the Company’s scrips by the Designated Persons of the Company including their immediate relatives will remain closed from 18th March 2026 to 27th March 2026 (both days inclusive), in terms of the Company’s Code of Conduct to regulate, monitor and report trading by Designated Persons, read with SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For TVS Holdings Limited R Raja Prakash Company Secretary Website: www.tvsholdings.com Email: corpsec@tvsholdings.com CIN: L64200TN1962PLC004792

Upcoming Board Meetings.pdf_text: To Date: March 18, 2026 The General Manager Listing, The Corporate Relations Department, BSE LIMITED PJ Towers, 25th floor, Dalal Street, Mumbai -400 001 Dear Sir/ Madam, Sub: Prior intimation of forthcoming meeting of the Board of Directors under regulation 29 of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. REF: S.M. GOLD LIMITED (Scrip Code: 542034) In accordance with Regulation 29 and other relevant provisions of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, we wish to inform you that a meeting of the Board of Directors of S.M. Gold Limited is scheduled to be held on Monday, March 23, 2026 at the Company’s Registered Office, situated at Shop No. 1 to 3, 2nd Floor, 24 Caret Building, Opp. Rock Regency Hotel, Law Garden, C G Road, Ahmedabad, Gujarat, 380009. As a reference to the meeting held on November 13, 2025 and February 17, 2026, where the Board approved the raising of funds through the issuance of fully paid-up equity shares by way of a rights issue, In the upcoming meeting the Board of Directors will re consider the terms and conditions of the Draft Letter of Offer including matters connected there to and related documentation along with Any other business with the permission of Chair. Further, as per the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015 and the Company's Code of Conduct, the Trading Window for dealing in the securities of the Company for all the directors/officers/designated persons of the company shall remain closed with immediate effect till the expiry of 48 hours after the conclusion of the Board meeting. For, S. M. GOLD LIMITED Pulkitkumar Sureshbhai Shah Managing Director (DIN: 07878190) CIN: L74999GJ2017PLC098438 Registered Office: Shop No. 1 to 3, 2nd Floor, 24 Caret Building, Opp. ROK Regency Hotel, Law Garden, C G Road, Ahmedabad-380009 Website: www.smgoldltd.com E-mail: compliancesmgold@gmail.com

Upcoming Board Meetings.headline: Outcome of the Board Meeting held on 18th March 2026
Upcoming Board Meetings.pdf_text: Ref: BHL/ STEX 69/ 2025-26 Date: March 18, 2026 BSE Limited National Stock Exchange of India Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers 5th Floor, Exchange Plaza, Dalal Street Bandra Kurla Complex Bandra (East) Mumbai – 400001 Mumbai-400051 Scrip Code: 539872 Symbol: BAJAJHCARE Sub: Outcome of Board Meeting - Allotment of Equity Shares pursuant to Conversion of Warrants under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 Dear Sir/Madam, Pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, (“Listing Regulations”) and in continuation to our earlier intimation dated September 19, 2024 regarding allotment of 20,79,409 fully Convertible Equity Warrants (“Warrants”) at an issue price of ₹338/- per Warrant to 8 allottees (Promoter, Promoter Group and Person(s) belonging to the Non-Promoter Public Category) with a right to the Warrant holders to apply for and be allotted 1 (One) Equity Share of the face value of ₹5/- each of the Company at a Premium of ₹333/- each for a period 18 months from the date of allotment of Warrants against the receipt of initial subscription amount of 25% of the issue price. In this regard, we wish to inform you that the Board of Directors at its meeting held today i.e. March 18, 2026, has considered and approved the allotment of 20,79,409 fully Paid-up Equity Shares of the Company of face value of ₹5/- each (“the Equity Shares”) to the Promoter, Promoter Group and Person(s) belonging to the Non-Promoter Public Category, upon the exercise of the option for Conversion of Warrants into Equity Shares at a price of ₹338/- per Equity Share (including a premium of ₹333/-) for each Convertible Warrant, pursuant to the Special Resolution passed by Shareholders at the Extra- Ordinary General Meeting held on August 07, 2024 in accordance with the SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018. In accordance with the terms of the issue of warrants,

Warrant Conversions.headline: Revision on Notification dated 17.03.2026 - Impact of Ongoing Geopolitical Situation
Warrant Conversions.pdf_text: Date: 18th March 2026 Listing Compliance Department BSE Limited The National Stock Exchange of India Ltd. Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Tower, Exchange Plaza, Plot No. C/1, G Block, Bandra Dalal Street, Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai – 400001 Mumbai – 400051 Scrip Code: 544198 Symbol: DEEDEV Subject: Revision of Market Notification, dated 17.03.2026 [Intimation pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015: Impact of Ongoing Geopolitical Situation and consequent Government Directives on Export Orders and Domestic Customer Supply] Dear Sir / Madam, We write to you in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. The purpose of this intimation is to keep our shareholders and the Exchange(s) informed of certain developments that have a bearing on our export operations and domestic supply commitments in the near term. As is widely known, the military conflict that broke out in West Asia in late February 2026 has led to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical maritime passages. The closure of this route has set off a chain of consequences that extend well beyond the immediate region, affecting shipping schedules, freight logistics, and energy supply chains across a large part of the world. Indian industry, as an integrated participant in global trade, has not been insulated from these effects. For DEE Development Engineers, the immediate consequence has been on our export orders to customers in the West Asia region. Certain shipments that were scheduled for execution in the near term are now at risk of delay, a situation that falls squarely within the ambit of Force Majeure under the terms of our export contracts. There is no possibility of cancellation of these contracts, and we remain confident of fulfilling our obligations. We have communicated formally with our affected overseas buyers in this regard and are working closel

Operational Alerts.pdf_text: March 18, 2026 To, To, BSE Limited, Listing Department, 25, P. J. Towers, National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., Dalal Street, Exchange Plaza, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai – 400 001 Bandra (East), Mumbai- 400051 Ref: Company Scrip Code: 532834 Ref: Symbol: CAMLINFINE || Series: EQ Sub: Disclosure under Regulation 30 and Schedule III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 (“Listing Regulations”): Opening of Judicial Liquidation Proceedings of CFS Europe S.p.A., a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company. Dear Sir/Madam, This is in furtherance to our intimation to the stock exchanges dated January 16, 2026 regarding an appeal filed by the Collegio Sindacale (Board of Statutory Auditors) of CFS Europe S.p.A. on January 15, 2026 with the Ordinary Court of Ravenna, Italy (‘’Court’’) to initiate liquidation proceedings for CFS Europe S.p.A., a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company. In this regard, we would like to inform you that the Court, through its order received on March 17, 2026, has taken a decisive step by initiating judicial liquidation proceedings for CFS Europe S.p.A., facilitating a structured and transparent resolution process. The proceedings have been initiated under the Italian Code of Corporate Crisis and Insolvency, following the aforesaid appeal filed by the Board of Statutory Auditors. The Court has appointed a delegated Judge and Court-appointed Liquidators (Curatori) for management of the procedure. Further updates, if any, will be provided as and when there are material developments. Disclosure under Regulation 30 read with Part A of Schedule III of Listing Regulations read with SEBI Master Circular No. SEBI/HO/CFD/PoD2/CIR/P/0155 dated November 11, 2024 is enclosed as Annexure 1. This intimation is also being uploaded on the website of the Company at https://www.camlinfs.com/. This is for your information and record. Thanking you, Yours faithfully, For Camlin Fine Sciences Limited Rahul Sawale Company Secreta

The above-mentioned Press Release is also being uploaded on the website of the company i.e. www.apisindia.com. You are requested to kindly take the above information on your records. Thanking You, Yours Faithfully, For Apis India Limited Amit Anand (Managing Director) DIN; 00951321 Encl: a/aApis India Limited’s Misk launches Masala Dates - Seedless, Superfood-On-The-Go in Bold Indian Flavours ● Seedless, sliced dates tossed in achari, imli (tamarind) and chilli-lime masalas; a flavour-forward, ready-to-eat alternative that is set to change everyday snacking. ● Soon to be available PAN India through retail stores and major e-commerce platforms. Hyderabad, 18 March 2026: Apis India Limited’s MISK has launched its newest innovation - Masala Dates in Hyderabad. It's a savoury spin on a traditional superfood-on-the-go that brings three punchy Indian masala profiles - Achari, Imli (tamarind) and Chilli-Lime to deseeded, sliced dates. Traditionally consumed in their whole form, dates often require preparation such as deseeding or cutting before consumption. Recognising this friction and the growing consumer interest in flavour experimentation, Apis India has introduced Masala Dates as a value-added, sweet-savoury innovation in the category. The range is designed for immediate consumption and flavour exploration, offering an alternative to sugary and fried snacks. Unlike conventional date products that largely remain in their natural sweet format, Masala Dates is an innovation that introduces a differentiated flavour dimension. It combines th

Ref: Symbol: TMB / Scrip Code: 543596 Dear Sir/Madam, Sub: Intimation under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 – Monetary Demand raised by the Assessment Unit, Income Tax Department This is to inform that the Assessment Unit, Income Tax Department, issued a Notice of Demand under Section 156 of the Income Tax Act, 1961, vide its Notice no. Thanking you Yours faithfully, For eMudhra Limited Johnson Xavier Company Secretary & Compliance Officer Membership No. A28304 eMudhra Limited eMudhra Digital Campus, 12-Pl-A & 12-Pl-B, Hi-Tech Defence and Aerospace Park (IT sector), Jala Hobli, B.K. Palya, Bengaluru, Karnataka 562149 Phone: +91 80 4848 4001 I Email: corporate@emudhra.comIWeb:www.emudhra.com J CIN -l72900KA2008PLC060368PRESS RELEASE Is Post-Quantum Cryptography the Next Y2K Moment for Global Digital Infrastructure? Are enterprises ready for a Y2K-scale disruption as post-quantum cryptography reshapes digital security? BENGALURU, KARNATAKA, INDIA, March 18, 2026 / EINPresswire.com/ -- eMudhra today raised a critical question for global enterprises, governments, and infrastructure operators: could the transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) represent a “Y2K moment” for digital security? As advances in quantum computing threaten to break widely used encryption standards, organizations worldwide face the challenge of replacing cryptographic systems that underpin financial transactions, identity systems, digital signatures, and secure communications. Governments and regulators in the United States and Europe have already begun pushing organizations toward quantum-resistant cryptography to protect long-term data and critical infrastructure. Press Releases.pdf_text: 119 Andheri Industrial Estate off Ve era Desai Road, Andheri West Mumbai 400053, India info@accedere.io https://accedere.io CIN L32000MH1983PLC030400 Date: 18th March 2026 To The Listing Compliance Department BSE Limited Phiroze Jeejeebhoy Towers Dalal Street Mumbai – 400001 Subject: Corporate Announcement Cyber Resilience, Data Privacy and AI: Why India’s Regulatory Shift Is Creating a New Compliance Technology Market Against the evolving regulatory backdrop, Accedere Ltd has been expanding its focus on AI-enabled Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) solution through its platform Controllo.ai. India’s digital infrastructure is undergoing rapid expansion, bringing with it greater regulatory attention to cybersecurity resilience and personal data protection. Two regulatory developments are particularly shaping this landscape: the strengthening of cyber resilience requirements under SEBI Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) and the implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA). While these frameworks address different dimensions of digital governance, together they signal a broader regulatory shift. Organizations are now expected to demonstrate not only strong cybersecurity defenses but also transparent governance over how personal data is collected, processed, and protected. This convergence of cyber resilience and privacy compliance is creating growing demand for technology-driven governance platforms capable of managing complex regulatory requirements.119 Andheri Industrial Estate off Ve era Desai Road, Andheri West Mumbai 400053, India info@accedere.io https://accedere.io CIN L32000MH1983PLC030400 A Convergence of Cybersecurity and Privacy Regulation Historically, cybersecurity compliance and privacy regulation were often managed as separate functions within organizations. However, newer regulatory frameworks increasingly require an integrated approach. SEBI’s CSCRF framework places strong emphasis on cyber risk management, r

Press Releases.headline: Emergency Disclosure - Unauthorised Filings on MCA Portal and Attempted Misrepresentation of Management Authority.
Press Releases.pdf_text: Date: 11 February 2026 Subject: Clarification and Denial of Unauthorized Appointment / Cessation of Directors Reflected on MCA Records Pursuant to Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, Unistar Multimedia Limited (“the Company”) hereby informs the Stock Exchange and the investing public as under: Unauthorized Filings on MCA Portal The Company has become aware that certain e-Forms DIR-12 have been filed on the MCA portal without the knowledge, authority, consent, or ratification of the Company or its Board, reflecting cessation of existing directors and appointment of new directors, without following the mandatory provisions of the Companies Act, 2013. No Board or Shareholder Approval The Company categorically states that: o No Board Meeting was convened for approving any resignation, cessation, or appointment of directors. o No notice of resignation was received from the concerned directors. o No shareholders’ approval was sought or granted. o No consent letters, disclosures, or statutory declarations were furnished by the so-called appointees. False and Void Actions Any appointment or cessation reflected on MCA records is: o Void ab initio, o Non est in law, and o Not binding on the Company or its legitimate Board, as the same has been carried out without authority, due process, or compliance with Sections 117, 118, 152, 168, 170, 173 and 447 and allied provisions of the Companies Act, 2013. Status of Existing Directors The Company confirms that the following five directors continue to remain validly appointed and in office, and none of them has resigned or been removed in accordance with law: o Mr. Vivekkumar Maheshkumar Padhiyar o Mr. Abhishek Pratpkumar Thakkar o Ms. Rupinder Manjotsingh Oberoi o Mr. Kiritbhai Solanki o Mr. Patel Vaibhavkumar AnilbhaiAny contrary representation is denied in toto. Actions Already Taken The Company and the aggrieved directors have: o Lodged formal objections and representatio

summaries.Acquisitions & M&A:
- **SEPC Limited (SEPC)** — The board has scheduled a meeting on 23 March 2026 to consider and approve the proposed acquisition of Avenir International.
📊 **Impact:** Positive (tentative) — The company may soon buy another business, which could help it grow, but we don't know the price or size yet, so it's too early to get excited.
👀 **Watch:** What price SEPC agrees to pay for Avenir International and whether it makes sense for the size of SEPC's own business.

---

- **Power & Instrumentation (Gujarat) Limited (PIGL)** — PIGL has acquired an additional 8.94% stake in Peaton Electrical Company Limited, though the deal value is not disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral to Positive — PIGL is tightening its grip on a company it already partly owns, like buying more shares in a business you already co-own — could mean it sees good future potential there.
👀 **Watch:** Whether PIGL eventually moves to acquire full control of Peaton Electrical, which would be a much bigger step.

---

- **G R Infraprojects Limited (GRINFRA)** — GRINFRA has filed a disclosure under SEBI's Takeover Regulations (Regulation 10(5)), indicating a substantial acquisition of shares has taken place, though specific numbers are not available in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is a regulatory paperwork filing that a company must submit when someone buys a large chunk of its shares; it doesn't by itself mean anything good or bad has happened to the business.
👀 **Watch:** Who has been accumulating GRINFRA shares and whether their stake crosses 25%, which would trigger a mandatory open offer to all shareholders.

---

- **Varun Beverages Limited (VBL)** — VBL's South African subsidiary, The Beverage Company Proprietary Limited, has acquired 100% of Twizza Proprietary Limited, a South African beverage company.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — VBL just bought an entire drinks company in South Africa, which means it can sell more of its products to more people there — think of it like opening many new shops overnight in a new country.
👀 **Watch:** How much revenue Twizza adds to VBL's next quarterly results and whether the South Africa business starts turning a profit quickly.

---

- **UGRO Capital Limited (UGROCAP)** — UGROCAP has formally closed the Share Purchase Agreement for the acquisition of Datasigns Technologies Private Limited, a fintech/data analytics company.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — UGROCAP, which gives loans to small businesses, has now fully bought a technology company that reads data smartly — this could help it decide who to lend to more accurately and avoid bad loans.
👀 **Watch:** Whether UGROCAP starts using Datasigns' technology to visibly reduce its bad loan (NPA) numbers over the next two to three quarters.

---

- **Sarda Proteins Ltd (519242)** — An open offer has been publicly announced under SEBI Takeover Regulations 3(1) and 4 by Grow House Wealth Management Pvt Ltd acting as manager, signalling that an acquirer wants to buy shares from existing public shareholders.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — When someone launches an open offer, they are offering to buy your shares, usually at a price higher than the
summaries.Financial Results:
## 📋 Financial Results — Pre-Market Briefing

---

- **Gaudium IVF and Women Health Ltd (544709)** — The company filed its unaudited standalone and consolidated financial results for Q3 and the nine months ended December 31, 2025, with the stock exchange.
- 📊 **Impact:** Neutral — routine compliance filing, no effect on stock; actual numbers were not provided in the announcement extract, so no financial performance can be assessed.
- 👀 **Watch:** Whether the detailed Q3 results show growth in patient volumes and revenue from IVF procedures compared to the same period last year.

---

- **ICICI Bank Ltd. (ICICIBANK)** — The Board of Directors has scheduled a meeting on **April 18, 2026** to consider and approve audited financial results for the full year.
- 📊 **Impact:** Neutral — this is just a calendar announcement saying "we will announce our report card on this date"; the actual results aren't out yet, so nothing changes for the stock today.
- 👀 **Watch:** The net interest margin and loan growth numbers when results are published on April 18, as these will signal whether the bank is earning more from lending in a high-interest environment.

---

- **Dharti Proteins Ltd (531171)** — The company's Board approved unaudited standalone financial results along with a limited review report for Q3 and nine months ended December 31, 2025.
- 📊 **Impact:** Neutral — routine compliance filing, no effect on stock; no specific revenue or profit figures were shared in the announcement extract to judge performance.
- 👀 **Watch:** Whether profit margins in the proteins/agro-processing segment have held up against rising raw material costs in the nine-month numbers.

---

- **Milestone Furniture Ltd (541337)** — The company filed a financial results announcement with the attachment referenced as "As per Attachment," with no figures or details provided in the headline extract.
- 📊 **Impact:** Neutral — routine compliance filing, no effect on stock; the announcement provides zero financial detail, making it impossible to assess business performance.
- 👀 **Watch:** The actual revenue and profit figures in the attached filing to see if furniture demand held up given the ongoing slowdown in discretionary consumer spending.

---

- **Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd (CLEANMAX)** — The company released the audio and video recording of its earnings conference call discussing unaudited standalone and consolidated results for Q3 and nine months ended December 31, 2025.
- 📊 **Impact:** Neutral — this is simply the company making its earnings call recording publicly available, which is good for transparency but contains no new financial data in this announcement.
- 👀 **Watch:** Commentary from management in the earnings call recording about new renewable energy project wins and whether order execution timelines are on track.

---

- **TVS Motor Company Ltd. (TVSMOTOR)** — The Board has convened a meeting on **March 24, 2026**, among other things, to consider and declare an **interim dividend** for the financial year ending March 31, 2026.
- 📊 **Impact:** Positive — when a company declares a dividend, it is literally sharing cash with shareholders; this is like a company saying "we made good money, here's your share of it," which usually makes investors happy.
- 👀 **Watch:** The exact interim dividend amount to be announced on March 24 — specifically whether it is higher
summaries.Mergers & Schemes:
• **Natco Pharma (NATCOPHARM)** — The board has scheduled a meeting on 24 March 2026 to consider and approve a demerger scheme, which would split one or more businesses out of the company into a separate entity.
📊 **Impact:** Positive (tentatively) — When a company splits itself into parts, existing shareholders usually get shares in the new company too, which can unlock hidden value; think of it like getting a bonus piece of a cake you already own.
👀 **Watch:** Which specific business segment is being demerged and whether the demerged entity will be separately listed on the stock exchange.

---

• **Orchid Pharma (ORCHPHARMA)** — The company is providing an update on the ongoing scheme to merge Dhanuka Laboratories Limited (the smaller company being absorbed) into Orchid Pharma Limited (the surviving company).
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — This is a progress update on a merger already announced earlier; no new deal or numbers have been disclosed, so nothing changes for shareholders today.
👀 **Watch:** The date on which the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) gives its final approval, as that is when the merger becomes legally binding.

---

• **Kirloskar Ferrous Industries (500245)** — The company has filed an update regarding its previously announced scheme of arrangement and merger, with no new financial figures disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — routine compliance filing, no effect on stock; this is simply the company keeping the stock exchange informed of where things stand, as required by rules.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the merger ratio (how many shares existing shareholders receive in exchange) gets revised or confirmed in the next regulatory filing.

---

• **ArisInfra Solutions (ARIS)** — The company has intimated the stock exchanges about a new scheme of amalgamation, whereby ArisUnitern RE Solutions Private Limited will be merged into ArisInfra Solutions Limited.
📊 **Impact:** Positive (tentatively) — ArisInfra is absorbing a related real-estate solutions company into itself, which could mean it is trying to bring more business under one roof and grow its revenue base; like a shop owner buying out a nearby supplier.
👀 **Watch:** Whether ArisUnitern RE Solutions brings meaningful revenue or profitable contracts that will actually show up in ArisInfra's quarterly results post-merger.

---

• **One Global Service Provider (514330)** — The company has received BSE trading approval for 1,24,38,296 new equity shares issued pursuant to the completed amalgamation of Plus Care International Private Limited with itself.
📊 **Impact:** Negative (mild) — Over 1.24 crore new shares are now free to be traded on the market; more shares in circulation means each existing share represents a slightly smaller piece of the company, which can gently push the price down.
👀 **Watch:** Whether these newly listed shares see heavy selling pressure in the first few trading sessions, which would indicate the original Plus Care shareholders are cashing out.

---

• **HDFC Bank (HDFCBANK)** — HDFC Bank filed a regulatory intimation (Ref. SE/2025-26/200, dated 18 March 2026) regarding ArisInfra Solutions' amalgamation of ArisUnitern RE Solutions; no figures relating to HDFC Bank itself were disclosed.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — routine compliance filing, no effect on stock; HD
summaries.Fund Raise / QIP / NCD:
• **CSL Finance Limited (CSLFINANCE)** — The board met on March 18, 2026 to approve raising fresh money by issuing Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs); exact amount not disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — the company is borrowing money through bonds to grow its lending business, which typically means it expects more loan demand ahead.
👀 **Watch:** The actual amount raised and the interest rate (coupon) at which these NCDs are issued — a lower rate means cheaper borrowing and better profit margins.

---

• **Purple Finance Ltd (544191)** — The company has redeemed (paid back) some of its unlisted, unsecured NCDs; exact redemption amount not specified in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — paying back borrowed money on time is normal and expected; it shows the company is meeting its debt obligations without any stress.
👀 **Watch:** Whether the company raises fresh debt soon after this redemption to fund new loans, which would signal continued business growth.

---

• **Power & Instrumentation (Gujarat) Limited (PIGL)** — The company has allotted new equity shares to investors by converting previously issued warrants under a preferential issue; exact number of shares and price not disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral to slightly Negative — new shares being added means existing shareholders now own a slightly smaller piece of the same company, like cutting a pizza into more slices.
👀 **Watch:** The price at which these warrants were converted into shares — if it is close to the current market price, dilution impact is minimal for existing investors.

---

• **QGO Finance Ltd (538646)** — The company has redeemed 550 unlisted, unsecured NCDs at a face value of ₹1,00,000 each, totalling ₹5.50 crore.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — repaying ₹5.50 crore of borrowed money on schedule is routine housekeeping; it means the company is financially disciplined and honoring its commitments.
👀 **Watch:** Whether QGO Finance raises a fresh round of NCDs at a similar or larger size in the coming weeks, which would indicate whether its loan book is growing.

---

• **Paisalo Digital Limited (PAISALO)** — The committee met to approve the allotment of NCDs pursuant to a prior intimation dated March 14, 2026; specific amount and coupon rate not disclosed in the headline.
📊 **Impact:** Positive — completing an NCD allotment means fresh funds have actually landed in the company's account, giving it more money to lend out and earn interest income.
👀 **Watch:** The total amount allotted and whether it matches the size announced on March 14 — any shortfall would suggest weaker-than-expected investor appetite for Paisalo's bonds.

---

• **Shalibhadra Finance Ltd (511754)** — The board of directors is scheduled to meet to consider issuing NCDs; no amount or timeline disclosed yet.
📊 **Impact:** Neutral — this is just a notice saying a meeting will happen; no money has been raised yet, so there is nothing concrete to react to right now.
👀 **Watch:** The outcome of the board meeting — specifically the size of the NCD issue and the interest rate offered, which will tell you how aggressively the company plans to grow.

---

• **Mac Hotels Ltd (541973)
summaries.Credit Ratings:
## Credit Ratings
